summary: PR 2749 focused on matching `lbmthp` data and related SObj/image descriptor data. The diff replaces several raw arrays, custom pair structs, integer casts, and address-named report/assert string symbols with typed `HSD_ImageDesc`, `HSD_SObjDesc`, `HSD_SObjDesc2`, real function signatures, `HSD_ASSERT`/`HSD_ASSERTREPORT`, inline `OSReport` strings, and corrected symbol sizes. The automated report shows three new data matches totaling +104 bytes, with one small code-score regression in `lbMthp_8001F410`.

key_files.role: Main data-matching cleanup: renamed `Movieplayer` to `MoviePlayer`, replaced local assert wrappers with `HSD_ASSERT` and `HSD_ASSERTREPORT`, inlined report strings, changed signatures and pointer types, and used `OSSecondsToTicks`/alarm callback typing.
Header surface updated for recovered `lbmthp` types/signatures; exact hunks not loaded in excerpt.
Static header reduced heavily, consistent with moving/recovering declarations and data definitions; exact hunk not loaded in excerpt.
Replaced scalar offset fields with embedded `HSD_ImageDesc` and `HSD_SObjDesc`, then passed typed descriptor pointers to `HSD_SObjLib_803A477C`.
SObj descriptor type definitions/prototypes updated; details not fully visible in loaded excerpt but used by multiple changed call sites.
evidence_refs: diff:typed uses of HSD_SObjDesc/HSD_SObjDesc2 in gm_1832, gmregtyfall, soundtest, lb_01F8

key_files.role: Small SObj library implementation adjustment; exact hunk not loaded in excerpt.
Converted a temporary `HSD_ImageDesc* descs[3]` array and integer cast into an `HSD_SObjDesc2` descriptor with named fields.
Removed local `ImageDesc_Pair` stand-in and used `struct HSD_SObjDesc` globals and fields for SObj creation.
Retyped extern `un_804D5990` from raw `char[]` to `HSD_SObjDesc` and removed an integer cast at the SObj creation call.
Retyped SObj descriptor global and unsigned data table; changed table literals from hex to decimal.
Changed a static table type from `int[]` to `u32[]`.
Touched for data matching; exact code hunks not loaded in excerpt, but bot report attributes new `.sdata2` and `.rodata` matches to this unit.
Related data/type cleanup; exact hunks not loaded in excerpt.
Related data/type cleanup; exact hunks not loaded in excerpt.
Small one-line data/type adjustment; exact hunk not loaded in excerpt.

what_changed: Changed 17 files with 227 added and 297 deleted lines.
Matched `main/melee/lb/lbmthp` `.sdata` from 14.29% to 100.00% according to the automated PR report.
Matched `main/melee/mn/mnstagesel` `.sdata2` from 88.24% to 100.00% and `.rodata` from 85.71% to 100.00% according to the automated PR report.
Corrected two GALE01 symbol sizes: `mnStageSel_803B8550` from 0x10 to 0xC and `lbl_804D3834` from 0xC to 0x8.
Replaced raw SObj/ImageDesc stand-ins and integer casts with `HSD_ImageDesc`, `HSD_SObjDesc`, and `HSD_SObjDesc2` in multiple call sites to `HSD_SObjLib_803A477C`.
Replaced custom `LBMTHP_ASSERT`/`LBMTHP_ASSERTREPORT` wrappers and address-named strings in `lbmthp.c` with `HSD_ASSERT`, `HSD_ASSERTREPORT`, and inline `OSReport` strings.

Adjusted function and data signatures in `lbmthp.c`, including `fn_8001EBF0` returning `size_t`, `fn_8001F2A4` taking `OSAlarm*`/`OSContext*`, and `lbMthp_8001F410` accepting typed `rate_table`, `buf`, and `heap_size` arguments.
Added `HSD_ASSERTREPORT` to clang-format whitespace-sensitive macros so formatting preserves macro-call layout.

smart_moves.text: The PR attacked data matching partly by improving type accuracy at the source level instead of preserving raw byte arrays or casts, especially around SObj descriptor construction.
Inlining ordinary report/assert strings in `lbmthp.c` removed address-named string references while preserving report text and assert line evidence.
The formatter configuration was updated for `HSD_ASSERTREPORT` alongside adopting that macro in source.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When data bytes around SObj descriptors do not match, check whether raw arrays or local pair structs are really `HSD_SObjDesc`/`HSD_SObjDesc2` and whether the callee can take a typed descriptor pointer instead of an integer-cast address.

Address-named string symbols in assert/report-heavy code can be generated residue; if the actual text is known, ordinary inline `OSReport` strings and `HSD_ASSERT`/`HSD_ASSERTREPORT` macros may be the source-level shape and can improve data matching.
A data-matching PR may accept a small local code-score regression when the evidence shows targeted data matches; here `lbMthp_8001F410` regressed by 3 bytes while three data items newly matched.

naming_conventions.name: MoviePlayer
naming_conventions.lesson: The PR changed `Movieplayer` to `MoviePlayer` for the lbmthp stream/player global; evidence supports only this local spelling choice, not a broader naming rule.

naming_conventions.name: HSD_SObjDesc / HSD_SObjDesc2 fields
naming_conventions.lesson: Use semantic descriptor field names such as `image`, `tlut`, and `image2` when the SObj descriptor type is recovered, instead of `x0`/`x4` or descriptor pointer arrays.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Replace integer casts to descriptor storage with typed pointer calls to `HSD_SObjLib_803A477C`.
Use real Dolphin/HSD helper forms for time and assertion/report operations (`OSSecondsToTicks`, `HSD_ASSERT`, `HSD_ASSERTREPORT`) rather than hand-expanded constants or wrapper macros.
Retype data arrays and globals (`int[]` to `u32[]`, raw char extern to `HSD_SObjDesc`, raw int global to `HSD_SObjDesc*`) when the data usage indicates unsigned or descriptor semantics.
Update symbol metadata sizes when matching reveals smaller owned data extents.

review_feedback.author: decomp-dev[bot]
review_feedback.text: GALE01 report showed matched data improving by +0.01% / +104 bytes, with three new data matches and one unmatched-code-item regression in `main/melee/lb/lbmthp` `lbMthp_8001F410`.

follow_up_queries: Search for remaining `(s32)` or `(int)` casts passed to `HSD_SObjLib_803A477C` that may hide `HSD_SObjDesc` descriptors.
Search for local pair structs shaped like `{ HSD_ImageDesc*; void*; }` that should be `HSD_SObjDesc`.
Search `lbmthp.c` for remaining address-named report/assert string symbols or raw `__assert` wrappers.
Inspect current `sobjlib.h` to confirm `HSD_SObjDesc2` naming and field layout are documented where descriptor call sites depend on it.
Search for `HSD_ASSERTREPORT` formatting regressions if clang-format whitespace-sensitive macro handling changes again.

notes: PR identity, state, changed-file list, and automated match report are strong.
The PR body was empty and there were no loaded human review comments, so reviewer intent and manual rationale are not available.
The loaded diff is truncated; several touched files are known only from changed-file metadata, not full hunks.
Automated report confirms data improvements and one code regression but does not prove why each source edit was chosen.
No claim is made that all changes are accepted global standards beyond what the loaded PR evidence and provided standards support.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Recover SObj descriptor types before preserving raw arrays or casts
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2749 repeatedly replaced raw descriptor arrays, char externs, local pair structs, and integer-cast descriptor addresses with `HSD_SObjDesc`/`HSD_SObjDesc2` typed storage and field accesses before calling `HSD_SObjLib_803A477C`.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Inline report/assert strings can be source-level data cleanup
accepted_candidate_records.text: In `lbmthp.c`, replacing address-named report/assert string symbols and custom assert wrappers with inline `OSReport` strings plus `HSD_ASSERT`/`HSD_ASSERTREPORT` coincided with the `main/melee/lb/lbmthp` `.sdata` item reaching 100%.

source_update_candidates.title: Keep `HSD_ASSERTREPORT` formatter-sensitive like `HSD_ASSERT`
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2749 added `HSD_ASSERTREPORT` to `.clang-format` `WhitespaceSensitiveMacros` while replacing local report/assert wrappers with `HSD_ASSERTREPORT` in `lbmthp.c`. Curator may consider whether formatting guidance should mention `HSD_ASSERTREPORT` alongside `HSD_ASSERT`.
source_update_candidates.reason: This is a source-owned formatter/standard detail; intake should not promote it as a global rule without curator review.

source_update_candidates.title: `src/melee/lb/lbmthp.c` data match involved inline strings and HSD assert/report macros
source_update_candidates.text: For `src/melee/lb/lbmthp.c`, PR 2749 changed custom assert wrappers and address-named string symbol uses to `HSD_ASSERT`, `HSD_ASSERTREPORT`, and inline `OSReport` strings; the bot reported `main/melee/lb/lbmthp` `.sdata` improved from 14.29% to 100%.
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential path-specific matching history for future lbmthp work.

source_update_candidates.title: SObj descriptor call sites were cleaned to `HSD_SObjDesc` typed pointers
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2749 recovered `HSD_SObjDesc`/`HSD_SObjDesc2` usage in several files and removed integer casts when passing descriptors to `HSD_SObjLib_803A477C`.
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential cross-path fact for future SObj descriptor cleanup; should be curated against current headers/source.

rejection_notes: Do not infer a broad rule that decimal table literals are always preferred over hex; the evidence only shows one `gmopening.c` table changed.
Do not infer that small code regressions are generally acceptable for data matches; this PR only documents one bot-reported tradeoff.
Do not promote exact `lbmthp.h`, `lbmthp.static.h`, `mnstagesel.c`, `mngallery.c`, `toy.c`, `mplib.c`, or `sobjlib.c` details beyond changed-file metadata because their full hunks were not loaded.
